decisions.title: Why File-Based JSONL Mailboxes
decisions.description: Each agent has a mailbox file in JSONL format (one JSON object per line). To send a message, an agent appends a line to the recipient's mailbox file. To receive messages, an agent reads new lines from its own mailbox. This is the simplest possible message-passing system: no message broker, no network protocol, no serialization library. JSONL is append-friendly, human-readable, and trivially parseable.
decisions.alternatives: A message queue like RabbitMQ or Redis Pub/Sub would provide ordering guarantees and acknowledgment semantics. However, these require infrastructure setup and add failure modes. For agents running on the same machine, file-based mailboxes are zero-dependency and sufficient.

zh.title: 为什么使用基于文件的 JSONL 邮箱
zh.description: 每个 Agent 拥有一个 JSONL 格式的邮箱文件（每行一个 JSON 对象）。发送消息时，Agent 向接收者的邮箱文件追加一行；接收消息时，Agent 从自己的邮箱读取新行。这是最简单的消息传递系统：没有消息代理、没有网络协议、没有序列化库。JSONL 适合追加写入、人类可读、解析极为简单。

decisions.title: Why Persistent Named Agents
decisions.description: Each agent in the team has a stable name (like 'coder', 'reviewer', 'tester') that persists across sessions. This allows agents to address messages to specific roles and build up expectations about each other's capabilities. A named agent can be stopped and restarted without losing its identity or its pending messages in the mailbox.
decisions.alternatives: Anonymous or dynamically spawned agents are more flexible but make coordination harder — who do you send a code review request to if there is no stable 'reviewer' identity? We chose named agents because team coordination requires stable identities, just like human organizations have roles.

zh.title: 为什么使用持久化命名 Agent
zh.description: 团队中的每个 Agent 都有一个稳定的名称（如 'coder'、'reviewer'、'tester'），跨会话持续存在。这使得 Agent 能够向特定角色发送消息，并建立对彼此能力的预期。一个命名 Agent 可以被停止和重启，而不会丢失其身份或邮箱中待处理的消息。
